_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: ASD with left to right shunt, associated with evidence of right ventricular volume overload without evidence of irreversible pulmonary vascular disease (Class I).
Asymptomatic child aged 2-4 years (Class I). For sinus venosus defect, surgery may be delayed to 4-5 years (Class IIa).
Symptomatic ASD in infancy (congestive heart failure and pulmonary artery hypertension) (Class I).
Elective closure irrespective of age if there is left-to-right shunt with right heart volume overload and pulmonary vascular resistance (PVR) is within operable range (Class I).
Symptomatic sequel of paradoxical emboli such as stroke or recurrent transient ischemic attack due to transient right-to-left shunting at the atrial level (Class IIa).

claim.label: Post procedure stills of ECHO with report
Invoice/ barcode of blade / device used

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure
Was the Echo/Doppler report suggestive of Atrial septal defect?
Was the Echo/color Doppler suggestive of other heart defect along with ASD?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Does the Post procedure still of ECHO show repair of the defect?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?
Is the Invoice/barcode of blade / device used submitted?

common_queries: Patient's Echo/Doppler report does not show Atrial Septal Defect.
Post-procedure ECHO stills do not show repair of the defect.
Echo/color Doppler report does not suggest other heart defects along with ASD, which is expected for this specific procedure.
Invoice/barcode of blade/device used not submitted at claim stage.

additional_information.special_empanelment: CCU/ICCU: Yes, Qualified cardiothoracic surgeon (MCh/ DNB): Yes
referral_criteria: Patients with borderline operability due to pulmonary vascular disease should be referred to a higher center for further evaluation.
contraindications: Severe pulmonary arterial hypertension or irreversible pulmonary vascular occlusive disease (Class III).
